friction_items.description: sys_tool_describe is broken in the modular refactor — calling it raises `NameError: name '_resolve_tool_name' is not defined`. The function lives in research_os/server/dispatch.py but handlers/meta_routing.py imports via `from .._handlers_runtime import *`, and _handlers_runtime does not re-export dispatch helpers. From a maintainer perspective this is exactly the class of regression Phase 10 was supposed to make easier to spot, and it shipped anyway. A grep across handlers/ for other dispatch-private symbols (_resolve_tool_name, _inject_consolidation_param) used post-wildcard-import would catch the family. Likely affects any tool a curious AI calls for self-introspection — first thing a new MCP client will try.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Add `from .dispatch import _resolve_tool_name, _inject_consolidation_param` to _handlers_runtime.py and write a 5-line preflight test that calls sys_tool_describe('tool_route'). Also grep handlers/*.py for any other symbol that resolves only through the dispatch module.

friction_items.description: tool_python_exec, tool_r_exec, tool_julia_exec, tool_bash_exec, tool_notebook_exec, tool_rmarkdown_render, tool_latex_compile, tool_paper_compile_typst, tool_wet_lab_plate_map_render, tool_engineering_fmea_render, tool_engineering_fault_tree_render, tool_snakemake_dag_render — 12 executor-shaped tools that could be one `tool_exec(language|format=...)`. Baseline flagged this and Phase 9 did not touch it. For RNA-seq specifically the AI was told by `tool_plan` to call `tool_python_exec OR tool_r_exec OR tool_julia_exec OR tool_bash_exec` — that branch lives in the description prose, not the tool surface, so it is invisible to a routing-only consumer.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Add tool_exec(language: enum, code: str, ...) as the dispatcher; keep tool_python_exec/tool_r_exec as aliases routed through it. Drop tool_julia_exec from the visible surface (never-called per baseline). Same pattern as the audit and dashboard consolidations that did land.

friction_items.description: No wet_lab/protocol/sequencing_data_acquisition was added. Greps for GEO / GSE / SRA / salmon / STAR / kallisto / fastq / nf-core / hisat across _router_index.yaml and the wet_lab pack are still empty. The biology RNA-seq prompt `download GSE181339, salmon quant, DESeq2 DE` semantic-routes to `guidance/analysis_plan` (0.829) with `methodology/bayesian_analysis` (0.686) as second pick — neither knows what GEO is. Baseline called this `the loudest scenario gap` and it is unchanged. For a maintainer pitching the wet_lab pack to a bio lab this is the first thing the prospect will type and the first place the tool will whiff.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Phase 16 / v2.1.0: add `wet_lab/protocol/sequencing_data_acquisition` covering GEO/SRA/ENA accession lookup, fastq download via sra-tools/pysradb, checksum validation, sample sheet generation. Add a `wet_lab/protocol/bulk_rnaseq_de` pipeline-skeleton protocol (DESeq2 / edgeR / limma) and `single_cell_rnaseq_qc` (scanpy / Seurat). Wire triggers for `GEO`, `GSE`, `SRA`, `fastq`, `DESeq2`, `scanpy`, `nf-core/rnaseq`, `salmon`. This unblocks the wet_lab pack pitch.

friction_items.description: tool_synthesize + tool_synthesize_plan + tool_synthesis_preview + tool_synthesis_curate_figures — 4 synthesis tools remain. Phase 9 collapsed audit (18→3), dashboard (8→1), search (7→1), figure (10→1), reviewer (5→1) — but synthesis was the largest convoluted block in the baseline and only saw partial work. From a maintainer adding a new output_kind (poster vs slides vs paper vs lay_summary), all four tools have to be considered.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Collapse to tool_synthesize(operation: enum {plan, preview, curate_figures, render}, output_kind: enum). Same dispatcher shape as the successful tool_audit / tool_dashboard / tool_figure consolidations.

friction_items.description: tool_plan + tool_plan_step + tool_plan_step_grounded + tool_plan_next_step (4 plan-shaped) AND tool_step + tool_step_complete + tool_step_literature_list + tool_step_pipeline (4 step-shaped) — 8 tools across the two families that ARE the project lifecycle. A new maintainer cannot infer when to call `tool_plan_step` vs `tool_plan_step_grounded` from the names alone; the doctrine lives in protocol bodies. Baseline flagged the plan family at 5-6 variants; we're down to 4. Step family is a NEW split introduced after consolidating step_pipeline.
friction_items.suggested_fix: tool_plan(operation: enum {turn, advance, step, step_grounded, next_step, clear}) is the obvious unification. Same for tool_step(operation: enum {finalize, list_literature, run_pipeline}). Move pipeline_define / pipeline_diagram / pipeline_run / pipeline_status under tool_step_pipeline as already done — extend the same pattern.

friction_items.description: _router_index.yaml is 2,026 lines (was 2,022 in baseline). No scripts/router_lint.py and no tool_route_explain were added. The maintainer still cannot answer 'why did this prompt route to guidance/analysis_plan instead of methodology/external_tool_setup?' without reading the YAML by hand. The semantic_candidates field in tool_route output is helpful (scores visible) but does not show which triggers matched or what tie-broke. Baseline's `scripts/router_lint.py` recommendation is unaddressed.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Ship scripts/router_lint.py that fails CI on (a) duplicate triggers across protocols without a tie-break, (b) router-index entries pointing at non-existent YAML, (c) protocols whose canonical example prompt does not land them in semantic top-5. Add tool_route_explain that surfaces matched_triggers + semantic_score + tie_break_path — maintainer-only flag.

friction_items.description: tool_search returned `{'status': 'success', 'data': {}}` for the PubMed query `DESeq2 PBMC differential expression review 2025`. No matched count, no advice, no warning that PubMed is unreachable or that the query parser failed. A maintainer triaging a user-reported 'search returns nothing' has zero signal from the response shape. Compare to the well-shaped tool_audit error that names valid scopes and points at the migration table.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Always populate `data.results_count`, `data.source`, `data.query_normalized`. On empty result, set advice to a fallback list ('Try widening date range / drop subject MeSH / check NCBI API key with sys_env').

friction_items.description: sys_active_tools fails with `protocol_name is required` if called bare. The MCP `instructions` field tells the AI to call sys_active_tools after tool_route — but in a fresh session before any protocol is loaded, the AI has nothing to pass. Maintainer-visible papercut.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Default protocol_name to the most recent entry in workspace/logs/protocol_log.jsonl OR to 'guidance/session_boot'. Return the recommended `active_tools` from the last tool_route call cached in .os_state.

friction_items.description: tool_context_intake + tool_intake_autofill + tool_intake_freshness — same three intake tools the baseline flagged, with the same unclear call order. mem_intake_regenerate is a fourth intake-shaped tool in the mem_ namespace.
friction_items.suggested_fix: tool_intake(mode: enum {autofill, refine, check_freshness, regenerate}). Document precedence (cold→autofill, after-answers→refine, every-N-turns→check_freshness) in the dispatcher description.

friction_items.description: tool_route + tool_quick_route + tool_semantic_route — three router entries. tool_route is the documented entry point; the other two duplicate parts of its surface. Maintainer pain when changing routing logic — three places to keep in sync.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Hide tool_quick_route and tool_semantic_route from the listed surface; keep them as _ALIASES into tool_route(mode=quick|semantic|hybrid).

friction_items.description: No prometheus/otel surface added. Phase 9 added structured deprecation telemetry inside aliases.py (good) but there is no maintainer-facing aggregator — no `tool_deprecations_summary` rollup, no router-miss top-N, no aggregated audit-gate fire rates across projects. tool_deprecations_summary EXISTS as a tool but it summarizes the deprecation log only, not maintainer-relevant signal.
friction_items.suggested_fix: v2.1.0: opt-in `~/.research-os/telemetry.jsonl` aggregator + `research-os telemetry summarize` CLI showing router miss rate, top unmatched trigger n-grams, average tool calls per protocol, audit-gate fire rates. Gated by researcher_config.telemetry_opt_in: true.

convoluted_tools: tool_plan vs tool_plan_step vs tool_plan_step_grounded vs tool_plan_next_step (4 plan-shaped tools — down from 5-6 in baseline but still split)
tool_step vs tool_step_complete vs tool_step_literature_list vs tool_step_pipeline (4 step-shaped tools)
tool_synthesize vs tool_synthesize_plan vs tool_synthesis_preview vs tool_synthesis_curate_figures (4 synthesis-shaped tools — Phase 9 did not consolidate this family)
tool_route vs tool_quick_route vs tool_semantic_route (three router entries — unchanged from baseline)

tool_python_exec vs tool_r_exec vs tool_julia_exec vs tool_bash_exec vs tool_notebook_exec vs tool_rmarkdown_render vs tool_latex_compile vs tool_paper_compile_typst vs tool_wet_lab_plate_map_render vs tool_engineering_fmea_render vs tool_engineering_fault_tree_render vs tool_snakemake_dag_render (12 executor-family tools — unchanged from baseline)
tool_context_intake vs tool_intake_autofill vs tool_intake_freshness vs mem_intake_regenerate (4 intake-shaped tools across 2 namespaces)

tool_slurm_estimate_cost vs tool_slurm_fetch vs tool_slurm_job_status vs tool_slurm_list vs tool_slurm_status vs tool_slurm_submit (6 slurm-prefixed pack tools that could be one tool_slurm dispatcher)
confusing_protocols: guidance/analysis_plan absorbs the biology RNA-seq routing (top semantic match) but the body says nothing about GEO accession download — AI is left to improvise the data-acquisition step
methodology/pick_tool_stack now embeds the DESeq2/scanpy/Seurat matrix in description (improvement from baseline), but routing precedence vs methodology/methodology_selection is still not arbitrated
wet_lab pack still has zero sequencing-acquisition protocol — pack pitch hits a coverage cliff at the first prompt
synthesis/* still has 19+ entries — no rationalization since baseline

literature/literature_per_step vs literature/literature_search vs literature/evidence_synthesis vs literature/systematic_review vs literature/comparative_paper_review — five literature protocols with unclear precedence, unchanged from baseline
never_called_tools: tool_humanities_essay_scaffold (still visible — humanities pack tool in biology surface)
tool_julia_exec (still visible — no canonical Julia RNA-seq lib)
tool_redteam_review (still visible — high-effort gate; skipped on first pass)
tool_alternative_path_propose (still visible — only fires on dead-end)
tool_humanities_archive_lookup / tool_humanities_citation_chain / tool_humanities_transcribe (humanities pack — never fires in a biology project)
tool_qualitative_codebook_diff / tool_qualitative_quote_provenance / tool_qualitative_select_standard (qualitative pack — never fires in a biology project)
tool_redcap_schema_describe / tool_synapse_entity_info / tool_cytoscape_export_static (single-tool packs; only fire when integrating those systems)
tool_nextflow_validate / tool_snakemake_dryrun / tool_snakemake_dag_render (only when nextflow/snakemake adapter is active)
tool_slurm_* (6 tools, only fire on HPC submission)
tool_paper_compile_typst / tool_latex_compile / tool_rmarkdown_render (only fires at final synthesis stage)
improvements_vs_baseline: Tool surface reduced from 163 to 146 (10% reduction). Not the <100 target from baseline, but real movement.

server.py refactored from a single 7,499-line file into 23 modules in src/research_os/server/ (largest is 579 lines). Maintainer navigation cost dropped dramatically — handlers/, tool_definitions/, registry, dispatch, envelopes are all separately reviewable.

Audit family collapsed 18+ tools into 3 (tool_audit + tool_audit_findings + tool_audit_quality_full). tool_audit(scope, dimension) is well-shaped and the error message names valid scopes and points at the migration table.

Dashboard family collapsed 8 → 1 (tool_dashboard). Search family collapsed 7 → 1 (tool_search). Figure family collapsed 10 → 1 (tool_figure). Reviewer family collapsed ~5 → 1 (tool_reviewer). These are the cleanest wins.

11 of 16 baseline never-called tools are now removed or hidden behind dispatchers (tool_search_arxiv, tool_dashboard_story_quality_bar, tool_dashboard_reviewer_sim, tool_audit_cliches, tool_figure_palette, tool_preregister_diff/freeze, tool_dead_end_lessons, tool_mistake_replay, tool_sensitivity_define/run).
MCP `instructions` field now names the boot sequence: sys_boot → tool_route → sys_protocol_get(format=summary) → sys_active_tools. New AI clients no longer have to discover this from prose.
sys_protocol_get default format flipped to `summary` — protocol body is now step_summary list of {id, name} pairs by default, saving substantial tokens per turn.
tool_route output gained `recommended_action` (a literal next-call string) plus `why_matched` (semantic score + intent class). Routing decisions are now self-explanatory in the response payload.
Every tool def now carries `status: live` and `pack: core|<pack>` fields. The 23 pack tools are clearly delineated from the 123 core tools, enabling a clean per-pack ON/OFF story.
117 of 118 protocols carry `scope_tags: {domain, audience, workflow_shape}`. Maintainer can now filter the router index by domain or audience.
methodology/pick_tool_stack hoisted the DESeq2/Seurat/scanpy/survival matrix into the description prose (visible at semantic-routing time), addressing the baseline 'doctrine invisible' complaint.
remaining_recommendations: v2.0.0 BLOCKER: fix the sys_tool_describe NameError regression — add `from .dispatch import _resolve_tool_name, _inject_consolidation_param` to _handlers_runtime.py. This breaks the very first thing a curious AI does for self-introspection. Add a preflight test.

v2.0.0 or v2.1.0: ship `wet_lab/protocol/sequencing_data_acquisition` (GEO/SRA/ENA accession lookup + fastq download + checksum + sample sheet). Add `wet_lab/protocol/bulk_rnaseq_de` (DESeq2 / edgeR / limma skeleton) and `single_cell_rnaseq_qc` (scanpy / Seurat). Wire triggers for GEO, GSE, SRA, fastq, DESeq2, scanpy, nf-core/rnaseq. This is the loudest scenario gap for the bio-lab pitch and was unaddressed by Phase 9–14.

v2.0.0: collapse the executor family. tool_exec(language: python|r|julia|bash, code: str) + tool_render(format: notebook|rmarkdown|latex|typst|plate_map|fmea|fault_tree|snakemake_dag). 12 tools → 2 dispatchers using the proven audit/dashboard/figure pattern.
v2.0.0: finish the synthesis consolidation — tool_synthesize(operation: plan|preview|curate_figures|render, output_kind: paper|poster|slides|...). 4 tools → 1 dispatcher.

v2.0.0: finish the plan + step consolidations — tool_plan(operation: turn|advance|step|step_grounded|next_step|clear); tool_step(operation: finalize|list_literature|run_pipeline). 8 tools → 2 dispatchers.
v2.0.0: hide tool_quick_route and tool_semantic_route as _ALIASES into tool_route(mode=quick|semantic|hybrid).
v2.0.0: collapse intake family — tool_intake(mode: autofill|refine|check_freshness|regenerate). Document precedence in the dispatcher description.
v2.0.0: collapse tool_slurm_* (6 tools) into tool_slurm(operation: submit|status|fetch|list|estimate_cost|job_status).
v2.0.0: fix tool_search silent-empty-payload — always populate results_count + source + advice; never return `{data: {}}` without an explanation.

v2.0.0: default sys_active_tools(protocol_name=None) to the last protocol in workspace/logs/protocol_log.jsonl. Today it errors hard on a fresh-session call that the MCP `instructions` field told the AI to make.

v2.1.0: ship scripts/router_lint.py (CI gate on duplicate triggers, orphan router-index entries, canonical-prompt regression) and tool_route_explain (matched_triggers + semantic_score + tie_break trace).

v2.1.0: opt-in anonymized telemetry — `~/.research-os/telemetry.jsonl` + `research-os telemetry summarize`. Router miss rate, unmatched-trigger n-grams, audit-gate fire rates, average tool-calls-per-protocol. Gated by researcher_config.telemetry_opt_in.

v2.1.0: tidy the never-called pack tools out of the default surface. tool_humanities_*, tool_qualitative_*, tool_theory_math_*, tool_engineering_*, tool_julia_exec — gate visibility behind the pack being installed or behind sys_active_project's domain field.
